queries.query: What controls apply to API security?

plan_steps.search_query: API security, protection of application programming interfaces and related authentication, authorization, input validation, and logging
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that address securing APIs including authentication/authorization, input validation, rate limiting, encryption, and monitoring/logging of API activity.

retrieved.title: Access Only
retrieved.description: Provide access from a single device to computing platforms, applications, or data residing in multiple different security domains, while preventing information flow between the different security domains.

retrieved.title: Assert and Enforce Application Access
Service Identification and Authentication
retrieved.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: system services and applications] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

retrieved.title: Interactive Application Security Testing
retrieved.description: Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to employ interactive application security testing tools to identify flaws and document the results.

retrieved.title: Authorization Process

queries.answer:
Applicable controls for API security from the supplied set:

- Require authenticating services and applications that provide or consume APIs: use Service Identification and Authentication to uniquely identify and authenticate system services and applications before they establish communications with devices, users, or other services or applications [ia-9]. This covers ensuring API clients and backend services authenticate to each other.

- Enforce and limit application access requested by APIs: require applications to assert the access they need and provide enforcement to prevent unauthorized access, and approve changes after installation, per the Assert and Enforce Application Access control [ac-3.12]. This applies to API-enabled applications asserting required accesses and having controls to prevent privilege escalation or unauthorized API access [ac-3.12].

- Isolate access across security domains when APIs bridge multiple domains: if a single device accesses platforms, applications, or data in different security domains via APIs, provide access only while preventing information flow between domains, per Access Only (Access Control) [ac-4.22]. This addresses APIs that could inadvertently transfer data across domain boundaries [ac-4.22].

- Test APIs for security flaws during development: require developers to use interactive application security testing tools and document results, per Interactive Application Security Testing [sa-11.9]. This applies to dynamic and interactive testing of API implementations to find vulnerabilities [sa-11.9].

- Manage API security through authorization and risk processes: include API-bearing systems and their environments in the organization’s authorization processes, assign roles and responsibilities in risk management, and integrate authorization into the enterprise risk program, per Authorization Process [pm-10]. This covers governance, approval, and ongoing authorization of API deployments [pm-10].

If you need controls for specific API capabilities (e.g., rate limiting, input validation, encryption in transit, logging/monitoring, token management, or endpoint protection) those are not covered by the supplied controls. The above only addresses authentication of services [ia-9], application access assertions and enforcement [ac-3.12], cross-domain access restriction [ac-4.22], interactive security testing [sa-11.9], and governance/authorization [pm-10].

queries.query: How should sensitive data be protected in cloud systems?

plan_steps.search_query: Protecting sensitive data in cloud systems through encryption and key management
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls requiring encryption of sensitive data at rest and in transit, and related cryptographic key management and protection requirements for cloud environments.

plan_steps.search_query: Access control and separation of duties for protecting sensitive data in cloud environments
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that enforce least privilege, multifactor authentication, privileged account management, and separation/partitioning to limit access to sensitive cloud data.

plan_steps.search_query: Data loss prevention, boundary protection, and monitoring for sensitive data in cloud services
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls addressing data loss prevention, network boundary protections, logging/monitoring, and data handling policies specific to cloud-hosted sensitive information.

retrieved.title: Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption
retrieved.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality and integrity of remote access sessions.

retrieved.title: Separation of Duties
retrieved.description:
a. Identify and document [Assignment: duties of individuals] ; and
b. Define system access authorizations to support separation of duties.

retrieved.title: Protection of Cryptographic Keys
retrieved.description: Cryptographic keys that protect access tokens are generated, managed, and protected from disclosure and misuse.

retrieved.title: Symmetric Keys
retrieved.description: Produce, control, and distribute symmetric cryptographic keys using [Selection: NIST FIPS-validated; NSA-approved] key management technology and processes.

retrieved.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
retrieved.description: Establish and manage cryptographic keys when cryptography is employed within the system in accordance with the following key management requirements: [Assignment: requirements].

retrieved.title: Cryptographic Protection
retrieved.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to [Selection (one or more): prevent unauthorized disclosure of information; detect changes to information] during transmission.

retrieved.title: Physical or Logical Separation of Information Flows
retrieved.description: Separate information flows logically or physically using [Assignment: organization-defined mechanisms and/or techniques] to accomplish [Assignment: required separations].

retrieved.title: Access Only
retrieved.description: Provide access from a single device to computing platforms, applications, or data residing in multiple different security domains, while preventing information flow between the different security domains.

retrieved.title: Boundary Protection
Separate Processing Domains
retrieved.description: Provide separate processing domains to enable finer-grained allocation of user privileges.

retrieved.title: Specialized Assessments
retrieved.description: Include as part of control assessments, [Assignment: specialized assessment frequency], [Selection: announced; unannounced], [Selection (one or more): in-depth monitoring; security instrumentation; automated security test cases; vulnerability scanning; malicious user testing; insider threat assessment; performance and load testing; data leakage or data loss assessment; [Assignment: other forms of assessment] ].

retrieved.title: Limits on Authorized Use
retrieved.description:
Permit authorized individuals to use an external system to access the system or to process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information only after:
  (a) Verification of the implementation of controls on the external system as specified in the organization’s security and privacy policies and security and privacy plans; or
  (b) Retention of approved system connection or processing agreements with the organizational entity hosting the external system.

retrieved.title: Data Sanitization
retrieved.description: When transferring information between different security domains, sanitize data to minimize [Selection (one or more): delivery of malicious content, command and control of malicious code, malicious code augmentation, and steganography-encoded data; spillage of sensitive information] in accordance with [Assignment: policy].

retrieved.title: Processing and Storage Location — U.S. Jurisdiction
retrieved.description: Restrict the geographic location of information processing and data storage to facilities located within in the legal jurisdictional boundary of the United States.

queries.answer:
Protect sensitive data in cloud systems by applying the supplied controls as follows:

- Encrypt sensitive data in transit and for remote access. Implement cryptographic mechanisms that protect confidentiality and integrity of remote access sessions and transmissions (use mechanisms that prevent unauthorized disclosure and detect changes) [ac-17.2] [sc-8.1].

- Establish and manage cryptographic keys and key protection. Define and implement key establishment and management in accordance with organizational key management requirements [sc-12]. Ensure symmetric keys are produced, controlled, and distributed using approved key-management technology and processes (e.g., FIPS-validated or NSA-approved as selected) [sc-12.2]. Protect cryptographic keys that secure access tokens from disclosure and misuse [ia-13.1].

- Isolate and control information flows and processing domains. Separate information flows logically or physically to achieve required separations between domains, and provide separate processing domains for finer-grained allocation of privileges [ac-4.21] [ac-6.4]. Allow access from a single device to resources across different security domains while preventing cross-domain information flow where required [ac-4.22].

- Protect network boundaries and place public components in segregated subnetworks. Monitor and control communications at external and key internal interfaces, implement subnetworks for publicly accessible components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks, and connect to external networks only via managed boundary protection devices per your security architecture [sc-7].

- Sanitize data moving between security domains. When transferring information across domains, sanitize data to minimize spillage of sensitive information and delivery or augmentation of malicious content per policy [ac-4.25].

- Limit and verify external system use. Permit external systems to access or process organizational information only after verifying the external system implements required controls or after retaining approved connection/processing agreements [ac-20.1].

- Enforce separation of duties and access authorizations. Identify and document duties and define access authorizations to support separation of duties so no single individual can improperly expose or misuse sensitive data [ac-5].

- Assess and monitor specialized risks. Include specialized assessments (announced or unannounced) such as in‑depth monitoring, automated test cases, vulnerability scanning, insider threat assessment, and data leakage or loss assessment as part of control assessments [ca-2.2].

- Constrain data location if required. If organizational policy requires U.S. jurisdiction for processing and storage, restrict cloud processing and storage to facilities within the U.S. legal boundary [sa-9.8].

If you need design details (specific cryptographic algorithms, key lengths, or vendor configurations), those are not covered by the supplied controls; the controls describe what must be done (encryption, key management, separation, boundary protection, sanitization, assessments, jurisdictional limits) but do not specify the low‑level parameters or procedures.

plan_steps.search_query: Access control requirements for information systems (account management, access enforcement, least privilege, separation of duties, session management)
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls across AC family that define account management, access enforcement, least privilege, separation of duties, and session/access session management requirements.

retrieved.title: Least Privilege
retrieved.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users (or processes acting on behalf of users) that are necessary to accomplish assigned organizational tasks.

retrieved.title: Access Enforcement
retrieved.description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

retrieved.title: Account Management
Structure for Least Privilege
retrieved.description: Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to structure security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware to facilitate controlling access with least privilege.

retrieved.title: Mandatory Access Control

queries.query: What policies relate to logging and monitoring?

plan_steps.search_query: logging and monitoring of information system events and audit records
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls requiring generation, protection, and review of audit logs and monitoring of system and user activity.

plan_steps.search_query: continuous monitoring and security event management
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls about continuous monitoring programs, security event detection, and centralized monitoring tools (SIEM).

plan_steps.search_query: audit log retention and protection
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls specifying retention, protection, and integrity of audit records and log review processes.

retrieved.title: Event Logging
Protection of Audit Information
retrieved.description:
a. Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion; and
b. Alert [Assignment: personnel or roles] upon detection of unauthorized access, modification, or deletion of audit information.

retrieved.title: Audit Record Retention
retrieved.description: Retain audit records for [Assignment: time period] to provide support for after-the-fact investigations of incidents and to meet regulatory and organizational information retention requirements.

retrieved.title: Logging and Review
retrieved.description:
(a) Log [Assignment: organization-defined audit events] for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; and
(b) Review the audit records of the maintenance and diagnostic sessions to detect anomalous behavior.

retrieved.title: Audit Record Generation
Integrated Analysis of Audit Records
retrieved.description: Integrate analysis of audit records with analysis of [Selection (one or more): vulnerability scanning information; performance data; system monitoring information; [Assignment: data/information collected from other sources] ] to further enhance the ability to identify inappropriate or unusual activity.

retrieved.title: Auditing Capability for Significant Events
retrieved.description: Upon detection of a potential integrity violation, provide the capability to audit the event and initiate the following actions: [Selection (one or more): generate an audit record; alert current user; alert [Assignment: personnel or roles] ; [Assignment: other actions] ].

retrieved.title: Continuous Monitoring Strategy
Continuous Monitoring
Audit Log Storage Capacity
retrieved.description: Allocate audit log storage capacity to accommodate [Assignment: audit log retention requirements].

retrieved.title: Cryptographic Protection
retrieved.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity of audit information and audit tools.

retrieved.title: Risk Monitoring
retrieved.description:
Ensure risk monitoring is an integral part of the continuous monitoring strategy that includes the following:
  (a) Effectiveness monitoring;
  (b) Compliance monitoring; and
  (c) Change monitoring.

retrieved.title: Automated Access Enforcement and Audit Records
retrieved.description:
(a) Enforce access restrictions using [Assignment: automated mechanisms] ; and
(b) Automatically generate audit records of the enforcement actions.

retrieved.title: Testing, Training, and Monitoring
retrieved.description:
a. Implement a process for ensuring that organizational plans for conducting security and privacy testing, training, and monitoring activities associated with organizational systems:
  1. Are developed and maintained; and
  2. Continue to be executed; and
b. Review testing, training, and monitoring plans for consistency with the organizational risk management strategy and organization-wide priorities for risk response actions.

retrieved.title: Trend Analyses
retrieved.description: Employ trend analyses to determine if control implementations, the frequency of continuous monitoring activities, and the types of activities used in the continuous monitoring process need to be modified based on empirical data.

queries.answer:
The supplied controls indicate these policies and policy-relevant requirements relate to logging and monitoring:

- Define what events are logged, why, and how often (event selection and rationale): [au-2] (AU-2a, AU-2c, AU-2d, AU-2e).  
- Specify which system components can generate audit records and who may select event types for those components; require generation of audit records with mandated content: [au-12] (AU-12a, AU-12b, AU-12c).  
- Allocate and manage audit log storage to meet retention requirements: [au-4].  
- Retain audit records for an organization-defined time period to support investigations and regulatory needs: [au-11].  
- Protect audit information and logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion, and alert designated personnel upon detection: [au-9] (AU-9a, AU-9b).  
- Use cryptographic mechanisms to protect integrity of audit information and tools: [au-9.3].  
- Log and review maintenance/diagnostic sessions for nonlocal maintenance and detect anomalies: [ma-4.1] (ma-4.1a, ma-4.1b).  
- Integrate audit-record analysis with other data sources (vulnerability scans, performance, system monitoring, etc.) to improve detection of unusual activity: [au-6.5].  
- Provide auditing capability and related actions when potential integrity violations are detected (generate audit record, alerts, etc.): [si-7.8].  
- Require organization-wide and system-level continuous monitoring strategies and programs that define metrics, monitoring/assessment frequencies, correlation/analysis, response actions, and reporting of security/privacy status: [pm-31] (pm-31a–f) and [ca-7] (ca-7a–g).  
- Include risk monitoring (effectiveness, compliance, change) as part of continuous monitoring: [ca-7.4].  
- Use trend analysis to adjust monitoring activities and frequencies based on empirical data: [ca-7.3].  
- Ensure testing, training, and monitoring plans are developed, maintained, executed, and reviewed for consistency with risk strategy: [pm-14] (pm-14a, pm-14b).  
- Enforce access restrictions with automated mechanisms and automatically generate audit records of enforcement actions: [cm-5.1] (cm-5.1a, cm-5.1b).

If you want, I can map these into specific policy statements (draft wording) derived only from the above controls.

queries.query: What are the requirements for multi-factor authentication?

plan_steps.search_query: multi-factor authentication for user authentication and remote access
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that require or define multi-factor authentication for user logons, privileged accounts, and remote access methods.

plan_steps.search_query: authentication factors and multi-factor configuration for privileged accounts
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that specify use of multiple authentication factors, enrollment, and management for privileged or administrative accounts (including enhancements).

retrieved.title: Access to Accounts —separate Device
retrieved.description:
Implement multi-factor authentication for [Selection (one or more): local; network; remote] access to [Selection (one or more): privileged accounts; non-privileged accounts] such that:
  (a) One of the factors is provided by a device separate from the system gaining access; and
  (b) The device meets [Assignment: strength of mechanism requirements].

retrieved.title: Multi-factor Authentication to Privileged Accounts
retrieved.description: Implement multi-factor authentication for access to privileged accounts.

retrieved.title: Device Identification and Authentication
retrieved.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: devices and/or types of devices] before establishing a [Selection (one or more): local; remote; network] connection.

retrieved.title: Access to Accounts — Replay Resistant
retrieved.description: Implement replay-resistant authentication mechanisms for access to [Selection (one or more): privileged accounts; non-privileged accounts].

retrieved.title: Authentication and Encryption
retrieved.description: Protect wireless access to the system using authentication of [Selection (one or more): users; devices] and encryption.

retrieved.title: Use of Alternate Authentication Factor
retrieved.description:
(a) Allow the use of [Assignment: authentication factors] that are different from the primary authentication factors after the number of organization-defined consecutive invalid logon attempts have been exceeded; and
(b) Enforce a limit of [Assignment: number] consecutive invalid logon attempts through use of the alternative factors by a user during a [Assignment: time period].

retrieved.title: Multi-factor Authentication to Non-privileged Accounts
retrieved.description: Implement multi-factor authentication for access to non-privileged accounts.

retrieved.title: Multiple System Accounts
retrieved.description: Implement [Assignment: security controls] to manage the risk of compromise due to individuals having accounts on multiple systems.

queries.answer:
The supplied controls require the following for multi-factor authentication (MFA):

- MFA must be implemented for access to privileged accounts [ia-2.1]. MFA must also be implemented for access to non-privileged accounts where specified [ia-2.2].

- For local, network, and/or remote access (as selected), one factor of the MFA must be provided by a device that is separate from the system being accessed, and that device must meet an assigned strength-of-mechanism requirement [ia-2.6(a)-(b)].

- Authentication mechanisms must be replay-resistant for access to privileged and/or non-privileged accounts as selected [ia-2.8].

- Wireless access must be protected using authentication (of users and/or devices as selected) and encryption, i.e., MFA for wireless access must be combined with encryption protections as defined under system selections [ac-18.1].

- Where primary authentication factors are exhausted after an organization-defined number of consecutive invalid logon attempts, the organization may allow use of alternate authentication factors; the organization must enforce an assigned limit on consecutive invalid attempts using the alternative factors within an assigned time period [ac-7.4(a)-(b)].

- Devices should be uniquely identified and authenticated before establishing local, remote, or network connections, which supports device-based factors in MFA [ia-3].

- The organization must implement assigned security controls to manage risks from individuals having accounts on multiple systems (which affects MFA deployment and account management) [ia-5.8].

If you need specifics not present here (for example: exact selections for local/network/remote, whether privileged/non-privileged, the required strength values for the separate device, exact replay-resistant methods, or numeric limits/time periods for invalid attempts), those details are not provided in the supplied controls.

queries.query: How should cryptographic keys be established and managed?

plan_steps.search_query: cryptographic key establishment and management
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls specifying requirements for establishing, distributing, storing, protecting, rotating, and retiring cryptographic keys and related key management practices.

retrieved.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
retrieved.description: Establish and manage cryptographic keys when cryptography is employed within the system in accordance with the following key management requirements: [Assignment: requirements].

retrieved.title: Asymmetric Keys
retrieved.description: Produce, control, and distribute asymmetric cryptographic keys using [Selection: NSA-approved key management technology and processes; prepositioned keying material; DoD-approved or DoD-issued Medium Assurance PKI certificates; DoD-approved or DoD-issued Medium Hardware Assurance PKI certificates and hardware security tokens that protect the user’s private key; certificates issued in accordance with organization-defined requirements].

retrieved.title: Symmetric Keys
retrieved.description: Produce, control, and distribute symmetric cryptographic keys using [Selection: NIST FIPS-validated; NSA-approved] key management technology and processes.

retrieved.title: Managing Content of PKI Trust Stores
retrieved.description: For PKI-based authentication, employ an organization-wide methodology for managing the content of PKI trust stores installed across all platforms, including networks, operating systems, browsers, and applications.

retrieved.title: Cryptographic Keys
retrieved.description: Provide protected storage for cryptographic keys [Selection: [Assignment: safeguards] ; hardware-protected key store].

queries.answer:
Use an organization-wide, documented key-establishment and key-management approach that covers generation, distribution, control, storage, and lifecycle actions for all cryptographic keys used by the system. Specifically:

- Define the key-management requirements that the organization will follow for all cryptography in the system (establish what algorithms, key sizes, lifetimes, roles, escrow/backup, recovery, and procedures are required) as required by [sc-12].

- For symmetric keys, produce, control, and distribute them using the selected validated technology and processes — e.g., NIST FIPS-validated or NSA-approved key management tools/processes — per the organization’s selection in [sc-12.2].

- For asymmetric keys, produce, control, and distribute them using the selected mechanism in [sc-12.3] (for example NSA‑approved key management technology and processes, prepositioned keying material, or approved/issued PKI certificates and hardware tokens as specified in the selection).

- Protect key storage: ensure cryptographic keys are stored in protected storage as defined by the organization’s selection in [sc-28.3] (for example hardware-protected key stores or other assigned safeguards).

- For any PKI-based authentication, manage PKI trust stores consistently across all platforms (networks, OSs, browsers, applications) using an organization-wide methodology so trust anchors and CA certificates are controlled and consistent, per [ia-5.14].

If you need a concrete set of the specific selections or the detailed requirements (e.g., which of the selectable options to use, exact safeguards for protected storage, or the full set of key-management lifecycle parameters), those are not specified in the supplied controls and must be defined by the organization as the assignments/selections referenced in [sc-12], [sc-12.2], [sc-12.3], and [sc-28.3].

queries.query: What controls govern incident response and reporting?

plan_steps.search_query: Incident response planning, roles, and procedures for detecting, reporting, and handling security incidents
plan_steps.purpose: Find primary incident response controls that establish incident response policy, plans, roles, detection and handling procedures, and reporting requirements (e.g., IR family controls).

plan_steps.search_query: Requirements for incident reporting to external organizations and authorities, including escalation and notification timelines
plan_steps.purpose: Surface controls and enhancements that govern reporting incidents to external entities, law enforcement, and higher-level authorities and specify notification/escallation timelines and information to be reported.

retrieved.title: Incident Response Plan
Incident Handling
Policy and Procedures
retrieved.description:
a. Develop, document, and disseminate to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles]:
  1. [Selection (one or more): organization-level; mission/business process-level; system-level] incident response policy that:
    (a) Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and
    (b) Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and
  2. Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the incident response policy and the associated incident response controls;
b. Designate an [Assignment: official] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the incident response policy and procedures; and
c. Review and update the current incident response:
  1. Policy [Assignment: frequency] and following [Assignment: events] ; and
  2. Procedures [Assignment: frequency] and following [Assignment: events].

retrieved.title: Incident Reporting
retrieved.description:
a. Require personnel to report suspected incidents to the organizational incident response capability within [Assignment: time period] ; and
b. Report incident information to [Assignment: authorities].

retrieved.title: Incident Response Plan
retrieved.description: Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to provide, implement, and test an incident response plan.

retrieved.title: Breaches
retrieved.description:
Include the following in the Incident Response Plan for breaches involving personally identifiable information:
  (a) A process to determine if notice to individuals or other organizations, including oversight organizations, is needed;
  (b) An assessment process to determine the extent of the harm, embarrassment, inconvenience, or unfairness to affected individuals and any mechanisms to mitigate such harms; and
  (c) Identification of applicable privacy requirements.

retrieved.title: Security Alerts, Advisories, and Directives

queries.answer:
The supplied controls that govern incident response and reporting are the following:

- IR-1 establishes the overall incident response policy and related procedures, requires designation of an official to manage policy/procedure development and dissemination, and mandates periodic review and update of the policy and procedures. This provides the foundational governance for incident response activities and roles [ir-1].

- IR-4 requires implementing an incident handling capability consistent with the incident response plan that covers preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery; ties incident handling to contingency planning; incorporates lessons learned into procedures, training, and testing; and ensures consistent rigor and scope across the organization [ir-4].

- IR-6 governs incident reporting: it requires personnel to report suspected incidents to the organizational incident response capability within an organization-defined time period and requires reporting incident information to organization-defined authorities [ir-6].

- IR-8 requires development of an incident response plan that provides a roadmap, describes structure and organization, defines reportable incidents, provides metrics, designates responsibility, addresses incident information sharing, is reviewed/approved at an assigned frequency, and is distributed, updated, communicated to incident response personnel, and protected from unauthorized disclosure/modification [ir-8]. IR-8.1 adds specific plan content requirements for breaches involving personally identifiable information (process for notice, harm assessment and mitigation, and identification of applicable privacy requirements) [ir-8.1].

- SA-15.10 places a requirement on system/service developers to provide, implement, and test an incident response plan for the system/component/service they deliver, tying acquisition to incident response capability [sa-15.10].

- SI-5 requires receipt of external security alerts/advisories, generation and dissemination of internal alerts/advisories/directives to defined personnel/elements/external organizations, and implementation of security directives or notification of noncompliance—supporting timely awareness and notification related to incidents [si-5].

If you need specific assignments (e.g., the reporting time period, designated roles, approval frequency, list of authorities to report to), those are not specified in the supplied controls and must be defined by the organization per the placeholders in the controls.

queries.query: What are the requirements for security awareness and role-based training?

plan_steps.search_query: Security awareness and training for all users (general security awareness program)
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that define organization-wide security awareness training requirements for all users, including frequency, content, and methods (e.g., phishing, insider threat, recurring training).

plan_steps.search_query: Role-based security training tailored to specific duties and privileges
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that establish role-based training requirements for personnel with specific responsibilities or elevated privileges, including content, timing, and updates tied to role changes.

retrieved.title: Role-based Training
retrieved.description:
a. Provide role-based security and privacy training to personnel with the following roles and responsibilities: [Assignment: organization-defined roles and responsibilities]:
  1. Before authorizing access to the system, information, or performing assigned duties, and [Assignment: frequency] thereafter; and
  2. When required by system changes;
b. Update role-based training content [Assignment: frequency] and following [Assignment: events] ; and
c. Incorporate lessons learned from internal or external security incidents or breaches into role-based training.

retrieved.title: Practical Exercises
retrieved.description: Provide practical exercises in security and privacy training that reinforce training objectives.

retrieved.title: Suspicious Communications and Anomalous System Behavior
retrieved.description: Provide literacy training on recognizing suspicious communications and anomalous behavior in organizational systems using [Assignment: indicators of malicious code].

retrieved.title: Training Records
retrieved.description:
a. Document and monitor information security and privacy training activities, including security and privacy awareness training and specific role-based security and privacy training; and
b. Retain individual training records for [Assignment: time period].

retrieved.title: Cyber Threat Environment
retrieved.description:
(a) Provide literacy training on the cyber threat environment; and
(b) Reflect current cyber threat information in system operations.

retrieved.title: Developer-provided Training
retrieved.description: Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to provide the following training on the correct use and operation of the implemented security and privacy functions, controls, and/or mechanisms: [Assignment: training].

retrieved.title: Anti-counterfeit Training
retrieved.description: Train [Assignment: personnel or roles] to detect counterfeit system components (including hardware, software, and firmware).

retrieved.title: Testing, Training, and Monitoring
retrieved.description:
a. Implement a process for ensuring that organizational plans for conducting security and privacy testing, training, and monitoring activities associated with organizational systems:
  1. Are developed and maintained; and
  2. Continue to be executed; and
b. Review testing, training, and monitoring plans for consistency with the organizational risk management strategy and organization-wide priorities for risk response actions.

queries.answer:
The supplied controls require the organization to provide both general literacy/awareness training and targeted role-based training, with documentation, periodic updates, practical exercises, and incorporation of lessons learned. Specifically:

- Role-based training content and timing:
  - Provide role-based security and privacy training to organization-defined roles and responsibilities prior to authorizing access or performing duties, and thereafter at an organization-defined frequency; also provide training when system changes require it [at-3].  
  - Update role-based training content at an organization-defined frequency and after organization-defined events; incorporate lessons learned from internal or external incidents or breaches into role-based training [at-3].

- Practical reinforcement:
  - Include practical exercises in security and privacy training to reinforce objectives [at-3.3].

- Awareness topics:
  - Provide literacy training on recognizing suspicious communications and anomalous system behavior using organization-defined indicators of malicious code [at-2.4].  
  - Provide literacy training on the cyber threat environment and ensure current threat information is reflected in system operations [at-2.6].

- Training for specific supply-chain and acquisition roles:
  - Require system developers to provide training on correct use and operation of implemented security and privacy functions/controls as specified by the organization [sa-16].  
  - Train designated personnel/roles to detect counterfeit components (hardware, software, firmware) as assigned [sr-11.1].

- Records and retention:
  - Document and monitor all security and privacy training activities (both awareness and role-based) and retain individual training records for an organization-defined time period [at-4].

- Program-level planning and oversight:
  - Implement and maintain processes that ensure plans for testing, training, and monitoring are developed, maintained, and executed; and review those plans for consistency with the organization’s risk management strategy and priorities [pm-14].

If you need specific values (which roles get which training, the exact frequencies, events that trigger updates, indicators of malicious code, retention time period, or the content of developer-provided training), those must be defined by the organization—these controls require you to assign those parameters but do not specify them here [at-3], [at-3.3], [at-2.4], [at-2.6], [sa-16], [sr-11.1], [at-4], [pm-14].

queries.query: How should removable media and portable storage devices be controlled?

plan_steps.search_query: Removable media and portable storage device protection and control
plan_steps.purpose: Find NIST SP 800-53 controls that require restricting, labeling, encrypting, sanitizing, and tracking removable media and portable storage devices and related management actions.

retrieved.title: Media Use
retrieved.description:
a. [Selection: restrict; prohibit] the use of [Assignment: types of system media] on [Assignment: systems or system components] using [Assignment: controls] ; and
b. Prohibit the use of portable storage devices in organizational systems when such devices have no identifiable owner.

retrieved.title: Portable Storage Devices — Restricted Use
retrieved.description: Restrict the use of organization-controlled portable storage devices by authorized individuals on external systems using [Assignment: restrictions].

retrieved.title: Media Storage
retrieved.description:
a. Physically control and securely store [Assignment: organization-defined types of digital and/or non-digital media] within [Assignment: organization-defined controlled areas] ; and
b. Protect system media types defined in MP-4a until the media are destroyed or sanitized using approved equipment, techniques, and procedures.

retrieved.title: Portable Storage Devices — Prohibited Use
retrieved.description: Prohibit the use of organization-controlled portable storage devices by authorized individuals on external systems.

retrieved.title: Nondestructive Techniques
retrieved.description: Apply nondestructive sanitization techniques to portable storage devices prior to connecting such devices to the system under the following circumstances: [Assignment: circumstances].

queries.answer:
You should apply the supplied controls as follows.

- Restrict or prohibit use of specified media types on defined systems or components by applying appropriate controls, per the organizational selection in [mp-7] (Media Use). Also prohibit portable storage devices that have no identifiable owner from use in organizational systems [mp-7].

- Physically control and securely store organization-defined digital and non-digital media in organization-defined controlled areas, and protect those media until they are destroyed or sanitized using approved equipment, techniques, and procedures [mp-4].

- For organization-controlled portable storage devices used on external systems, either restrict their use by authorized individuals with the organization’s defined restrictions [ac-20.2], or prohibit such use entirely if that is the organizational decision [ac-20.5].

- Before connecting portable storage devices to systems, apply nondestructive sanitization techniques under the organization-defined circumstances (i.e., sanitize when those circumstances are met) [mp-6.3].

If you need specific assignments (the exact types of media, controlled areas, the selected restriction/prohibition options, the restrictions to apply to external systems, or the circumstances for nondestructive sanitization), those are not defined in the supplied controls and must be specified by the organization; the supplied controls only state the requirement types above.

queries.query: What controls address vulnerability scanning and flaw remediation?

plan_steps.search_query: vulnerability scanning and flaw remediation
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that require scanning systems for vulnerabilities and processes for remediating identified flaws (including vulnerability management, scanning frequency, reporting, and patching/mitigation actions).

retrieved.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
Flaw Remediation
Malicious Code Analysis
retrieved.description:
(a) Employ the following tools and techniques to analyze the characteristics and behavior of malicious code: [Assignment: tools and techniques] ; and
(b) Incorporate the results from malicious code analysis into organizational incident response and flaw remediation processes.

retrieved.title: Plan of Action and Milestones
retrieved.description:
a. Develop a plan of action and milestones for the system to document the planned remediation actions of the organization to correct weaknesses or deficiencies noted during the assessment of the controls and to reduce or eliminate known vulnerabilities in the system; and
b. Update existing plan of action and milestones [Assignment: frequency] based on the findings from control assessments, independent audits or reviews, and continuous monitoring activities.

retrieved.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation

queries.answer:
The supplied controls that address vulnerability scanning and flaw remediation are:

- Vulnerability scanning and monitoring: [ra-5] requires monitoring and scanning for vulnerabilities in systems and hosted applications at organization-defined frequencies and when new vulnerabilities are reported, use of vulnerability monitoring tools and standards (enumeration, checklists/test procedures, impact measurement), analysis of scan reports, sharing scan information, and use of tools that can be readily updated [ra-5].

- Flaw remediation (identification, testing, installation, CM integration): [si-2] requires identifying, reporting, and correcting system flaws; testing software/firmware updates for effectiveness and side effects before installation; installing security-relevant updates within an organization-defined time period; and incorporating flaw remediation into the organizational configuration management process [si-2].

- Malicious code analysis supporting remediation: [si-3.10] requires employing tools/techniques to analyze malicious code characteristics/behavior and incorporating those analysis results into incident response and flaw remediation processes [si-3.10].

- Planning and tracking remediation actions: [ca-5] requires developing and maintaining a Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M) to document planned remediation actions to correct weaknesses and reduce or eliminate known vulnerabilities, and updating the POA&M based on assessments and monitoring [ca-5].

- Developer responsibilities for remediation in acquisition/lifecycle: [sa-11] requires developers to implement ongoing control assessments, perform testing (unit/integration/system/regression as selected), produce evidence of testing, implement a verifiable flaw remediation process, and correct flaws found during testing/evaluation [sa-11].

If you need a concise mapping (e.g., which to use for scanning vs. patching vs. tracking), say so and I will provide it.

queries.query: What are the contingency planning and system backup requirements?

plan_steps.search_query: Contingency planning and continuity of operations requirements for information systems
plan_steps.purpose: Find NIST SP 800-53 controls that define contingency planning, continuity of operations, incident response, and related recovery planning requirements (e.g., CP family controls).

plan_steps.search_query: System backup and backup protection requirements for information systems
plan_steps.purpose: Find NIST SP 800-53 controls that define system and data backup, storage, restoration testing, and protections for backups (e.g., CP-9, CP-10, MP-5, and related controls).

retrieved.title: Contingency Plan
System Backup
Continue Mission and Business Functions
retrieved.description: Plan for the continuance of [Selection: all; essential] mission and business functions with minimal or no loss of operational continuity and sustains that continuity until full system restoration at primary processing and/or storage sites.

retrieved.title: Alternate Storage Site
retrieved.description:
a. Establish an alternate storage site, including necessary agreements to permit the storage and retrieval of system backup information; and
b. Ensure that the alternate storage site provides controls equivalent to that of the primary site.

retrieved.title: Capacity Planning
retrieved.description: Conduct capacity planning so that necessary capacity for information processing, telecommunications, and environmental support exists during contingency operations.

retrieved.title: Alternate Processing and Storage Sites
retrieved.description: Plan for the transfer of [Selection: all; essential] mission and business functions to alternate processing and/or storage sites with minimal or no loss of operational continuity and sustain that continuity through system restoration to primary processing and/or storage sites.

retrieved.title: Redundant Secondary System
retrieved.description: Conduct system backup by maintaining a redundant secondary system that is not collocated with the primary system and that can be activated without loss of information or disruption to operations.

retrieved.title: Coordinate with External Service Providers
retrieved.description: Coordinate the contingency plan with the contingency plans of external service providers to ensure that contingency requirements can be satisfied.

retrieved.title: Separate Storage for Critical Information
retrieved.description: Store backup copies of [Assignment: critical system software and other security-related information] in a separate facility or in a fire rated container that is not collocated with the operational system.

retrieved.title: Conceptually Simple Design
retrieved.description:
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to:
  (a) Design and structure the security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware to use a complete, conceptually simple protection mechanism with precisely defined semantics; and
  (b) Internally structure the security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware with specific regard for this mechanism.

queries.answer:
Contingency planning requirements
- Develop a documented contingency plan that: identifies essential mission and business functions and their contingency requirements; provides recovery objectives, restoration priorities, and metrics; assigns roles and contact information; addresses maintaining essential functions during disruption, eventual full system restoration without control degradation, and sharing of contingency information; and is reviewed and approved by organization‑defined personnel/roles [cp-2a.1][cp-2a.2][cp-2a.3][cp-2a.4][cp-2a.5][cp-2a.6][cp-2a.7].  
- Distribute plan copies to organization‑defined key contingency personnel and organizational elements [cp-2b].  
- Coordinate contingency planning with incident handling activities [cp-2c].  
- Review the contingency plan at the organization‑defined frequency and update it to reflect organizational, system, or environmental changes and issues discovered during testing/execution [cp-2d][cp-2e].  
- Communicate plan changes to key contingency personnel and organizational elements and incorporate lessons learned from testing, training, or actual events into future testing and training [cp-2f][cp-2g].  
- Protect the contingency plan from unauthorized disclosure and modification [cp-2h].  
- Plan for continuance of essential (or all, per selection) mission and business functions with minimal or no loss of operational continuity and sustain that continuity until full restoration at primary processing/storage sites [cp-2.5].  
- Conduct capacity planning to ensure necessary processing, telecommunications, and environmental support during contingency operations [cp-2.2].  
- Plan for transfer of essential (or all) mission and business functions to alternate processing and/or storage sites with minimal or no loss of continuity and sustain that continuity until restoration at primary sites [cp-2.6].  
- Coordinate the organization’s contingency plan with external service providers’ contingency plans so contingency requirements can be met [cp-2.7].

System backup requirements
- Conduct backups of user‑level information stored in the system components at the organization‑defined frequency [cp-9a].  
- Conduct backups of system‑level information at the organization‑defined frequency [cp-9b].  
- Conduct backups of system documentation, including security‑ and privacy‑related documentation, at the organization‑defined frequency [cp-9c].  
- Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of backup information [cp-9d].  
- Maintain an alternate storage site (with necessary agreements) for storage and retrieval of backup information, ensuring controls at the alternate site are equivalent to the primary site [cp-6a][cp-6b].  
- Store backup copies of critical system software and other security‑related information in a separate facility or in a fire‑rated container not collocated with the operational system [cp-9.3].  
- Where applicable, maintain a redundant secondary system that is not collocated with the primary system and can be activated without loss of information or operational disruption [cp-9.6].

Design consideration
- Require system developers to use conceptually simple, precisely defined protection mechanisms in security‑relevant hardware/software/firmware to support dependable contingency and backup behavior [sa-17.5a][sa-17.5b].

If you need frequencies, selections (e.g., whether to plan for “essential” vs “all” functions), or the identities of personnel and storage locations, those are organization‑defined and must be supplied to fully specify the plan and backup schedule; the supplied controls require those assignments but do not themselves define them [cp-2b][cp-9a][cp-9b][cp-9c][cp-2.6].

queries.query: How is least privilege enforced and separation of duties maintained?

plan_steps.search_query: least privilege enforcement for access to systems and data
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that require implementing least privilege principles, limiting access rights, and managing privileges for accounts and privileges assignment.

plan_steps.search_query: separation of duties and conflict of interest controls
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that require separating duties, enforcing role separation, and preventing conflicts of interest through role design, approvals, and compensating controls.

retrieved.title: Least Privilege
retrieved.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users (or processes acting on behalf of users) that are necessary to accomplish assigned organizational tasks.

retrieved.title: Structure for Least Privilege
retrieved.description: Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to structure security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware to facilitate controlling access with least privilege.

retrieved.title: Least Privilege
retrieved.description: Implement the security design principle of least privilege in [Assignment: systems or system components].

retrieved.title: Privilege Limitation for Production and Operation
retrieved.description:
(a) Limit privileges to change system components and system-related information within a production or operational environment; and
(b) Review and reevaluate privileges [Assignment: organization-defined frequency].

retrieved.title: Individuals Without Appropriate Access
retrieved.description:
(a) Implement procedures for the use of maintenance personnel that lack appropriate security clearances or are not U.S. citizens, that include the following requirements:
  (1) Maintenance personnel who do not have needed access authorizations, clearances, or formal access approvals are escorted and supervised during the performance of maintenance and diagnostic activities on the system by approved organizational personnel who are fully cleared, have appropriate access authorizations, and are technically qualified; and
  (2) Prior to initiating maintenance or diagnostic activities by personnel who do not have needed access authorizations, clearances or formal access approvals, all volatile information storage components within the system are sanitized and all nonvolatile storage media are removed or physically disconnected from the system and secured; and
(b) Develop and implement [Assignment: alternate controls] in the event a system component cannot be sanitized, removed, or disconnected from the system.

retrieved.title: Separation of Duties
retrieved.description:
a. Identify and document [Assignment: duties of individuals] ; and
b. Define system access authorizations to support separation of duties.

retrieved.title: Physical or Logical Separation of Information Flows
retrieved.description: Separate information flows logically or physically using [Assignment: organization-defined mechanisms and/or techniques] to accomplish [Assignment: required separations].

retrieved.title: Disassociability
retrieved.description: Implement [Assignment: measures] to disassociate individuals from audit information transmitted across organizational boundaries.

queries.answer:
Least privilege is enforced by assigning and limiting access so users and processes receive only the accesses required to perform their assigned tasks, and by building systems to support that restriction. Specifically, the organization employs the principle of least privilege for users and processes [ac-6]; requires developers to structure security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware to facilitate access control consistent with least privilege [sa-17.7]; and mandates that least-privilege design be implemented in systems or system components per acquisition requirements [sa-8.14]. In production and operational environments, privileges to change system components and related information are restricted and reviewed at an organization-defined frequency to maintain appropriate privilege levels over time [cm-5.5].

Separation of duties is maintained by formally identifying and documenting duties and then defining system access authorizations to enforce those separations [ac-5]. Where separation of information flows is required, the organization separates flows either logically or physically using organization-defined mechanisms or techniques to achieve the required separations [ac-4.21]. For situations involving maintenance personnel without appropriate clearances or access, controls ensure such personnel are escorted and supervised and that volatile storage is sanitized or nonvolatile media removed (or alternate controls implemented) to prevent unauthorized access or duties conflicts during maintenance activities [ma-5.1].

When audit information crosses organizational boundaries, measures are implemented to disassociate individuals from that audit information, supporting privacy and preventing duty conflicts tied to audit data handling [au-16.3].

If you need specifics (e.g., the organization-defined mechanisms for logical separation, the frequency for privilege reviews, or the exact alternate controls for unsanitizable components), those are not defined in the supplied controls and would need to be specified by the organization.

queries.query: What controls apply to configuration management and baseline configurations?

plan_steps.search_query: configuration management and baseline configurations
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that specify establishing, documenting, and maintaining baseline configurations, configuration change control, and configuration monitoring.

plan_steps.search_query: configuration management policy and procedures
plan_steps.purpose: Find controls that require policies, procedures, and roles for managing configurations and change control processes.

retrieved.title: Policy and Procedures
retrieved.description:
a. Develop, document, and disseminate to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles]:
  1. [Selection (one or more): organization-level; mission/business process-level; system-level] configuration management policy that:
    (a) Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and
    (b) Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and
  2. Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the configuration management policy and the associated configuration management controls;
b. Designate an [Assignment: official] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the configuration management policy and procedures; and
c. Review and update the current configuration management:
  1. Policy [Assignment: frequency] and following [Assignment: events] ; and
  2. Procedures [Assignment: frequency] and following [Assignment: events].

retrieved.title: Baseline Configuration
retrieved.description:
a. Develop, document, and maintain under configuration control, a current baseline configuration of the system; and
b. Review and update the baseline configuration of the system:
  1. [Assignment: frequency];
  2. When required due to [Assignment: circumstances] ; and
  3. When system components are installed or upgraded.

retrieved.title: Development and Test Environments
retrieved.description: Maintain a baseline configuration for system development and test environments that is managed separately from the operational baseline configuration.

retrieved.title: Configuration Management Plan
Automated Change Implementation
retrieved.description: Implement changes to the current system baseline and deploy the updated baseline across the installed base using [Assignment: automated mechanisms].

retrieved.title: User-installed Software
Automation Support for Accuracy and Currency
retrieved.description: Maintain the currency, completeness, accuracy, and availability of the baseline configuration of the system using [Assignment: automated mechanisms].

retrieved.title: Automated Documentation, Notification, and Prohibition of Changes
Configuration Settings

queries.answer:
The supplied controls that apply to configuration management and baseline configurations are the following (described in plain terms, citing each control):

- CM-1: Establish and maintain a configuration management policy and procedures, assign an official to manage them, and periodically review/update the policy and procedures [cm-1].

- CM-2: Create, document, and keep under configuration control a current system baseline configuration; review and update that baseline at a defined frequency, when certain circumstances require it, and when components are installed or upgraded [cm-2].

- CM-2.6: Maintain a separate baseline configuration for development and test environments, managed independently from the operational baseline [cm-2.6].

- CM-2.2: Use automated mechanisms to keep the baseline configuration current, complete, accurate, and available [cm-2.2].

- CM-3.1: Use automated mechanisms to document proposed changes, notify approval authorities and request approvals, flag unapproved changes within a time period, prohibit changes until approvals are received, record all changes, and notify personnel when approved changes are completed [cm-3.1].

- CM-3.3: Implement changes to the baseline and deploy the updated baseline across the installed base using automated mechanisms [cm-3.3].

- CM-6: Establish, document, and implement configuration settings that are the most restrictive consistent with operations (using common secure configurations); approve and document any deviations; and monitor/control changes to those settings [cm-6].

- CM-9: Develop, document, implement, and protect a configuration management plan that defines roles/responsibilities, processes for identifying and managing configuration items throughout the system life cycle, designates which items are under configuration management, and is reviewed/approved by designated personnel [cm-9].

- CM-11: Establish and enforce policies governing user-installed software and monitor compliance with those policies [cm-11].

These controls collectively cover policy/procedures, baseline creation and maintenance (including dev/test baselines), automation to maintain and implement baselines and manage change, configuration settings and approved deviations, configuration management planning, and user-installed software controls relevant to configuration posture [cm-1] [cm-2] [cm-2.6] [cm-2.2] [cm-3.1] [cm-3.3] [cm-6] [cm-9] [cm-11].
